back_translation: Therefore, since we have this ministry/mission according to the mercy we have received, we do not become discouraged.

On the contrary, we have abandoned shameful secret ways, neither walking with cunning, nor distorting the word of God, but with the open manifestation of truth, we commend ourselves before the conscience of every person, before the face of God.
And if our gospel is covered, it is covered for those who are perishing.

Among them, the god of this age has caused the minds of those who do not believe to become blind, so that they will not see the light of the gospel concerning the glory of Christ, who is the image of God.
For we do not proclaim ourselves, but proclaim Jesus Christ as the Lord, and ourselves as slave-servants of you, for the sake of Jesus.
For God who said, 'Light will shine out of darkness,' He himself shone in our hearts, so that there would be light of the knowing-of the glory of God in the face of Christ.
But we have this treasure in clay-fired vessels, so that this great mighty power belongs to God, not to us.
We are pressed in every direction, but not pressed until there is no way out; we lack wisdom/are at a loss, but we do not lose hope.
We are persecuted, but not abandoned; we are knocked down, but not destroyed.
We carry the death of Jesus in our body always, so that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our body.
For we who are still alive are always handed over to death because of Jesus, so that the life of Jesus may also be revealed in our mortal flesh.
Therefore, death is at work in us, but life is at work in you.
And we have the same spirit of faith, according to what is written, 'I believed, therefore I spoke,' we ourselves also believe, and therefore we speak.
For we know that He who raised Jesus to life will also raise us to life together with Jesus, and will present us together with you.
For all these things are for your sake, so that grace which is increasing through many people, will cause thanksgiving to overflow for the glory of God.
Therefore, we do not become discouraged. Even though our outer person is wasting away, our inner person is being made-new again every day.
For our momentary, light affliction is producing for us a burden of eternal glory greatly surpassing any comparison.
For we do not gaze intently at things that are visible, but we gaze intently at things that are invisible. For things that are visible are temporary, but things that are invisible are eternal.
